questions.question: In the following questions, a sentence has been divided into parts. Some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'. The farmer wanted to selling the (1)/ newly harvested corn and buy good things (2)/ for his family with the money. (3)/ No error (4)
questions.explanation: The base form of the verb 'sell' should be used with the infinitive 'to'. C is the right answer.

questions.question: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'. Her manners and conversation were so (1)/ pleasing to him that he spent the afternoon (2)/ and evening in her company. (3)/ No error (4)
questions.explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct and free of error. A is the right answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, four words are given, out of which only one word is correctly spelt. Find the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct spellings of the other words are: appendix, amendment and flamboyant. C is the right answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, a sentence is given in either direct or indirect speech. Select the option that correctly converts the sentence in direct speech to indirect speech or vice versa. The youngsters said that they desired to help their country.
options: "We desired to help our country," said the youngsters.
"We desire to help our country," said the youngsters.
"We had desired to help our country," said the youngsters.
"We will desire to help our country," said the youngsters.
questions.explanation: When converting indirect to direct speech, the simple past tense 'desired' should change to the simple present tense 'desire'. B is the right answer.

questions.question: In the following question, a sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active Voice. The occasional policeman is attracted by street performances of all types.
options: Street performances of all types will attract the occasional policeman.
Street performances of all types are attracting the occasional policeman.
Street performances of all types attracted the occasional policeman.
Street performances of all types attract the occasional policeman.
questions.explanation: The sentence is in passive voice and in simple present tense (is attracted). A is incorrect as 'will attract' is in simple future tense. B is incorrect as 'are attracting' is in present continuous tense. C is incorrect as 'attracted' is in simple past tense. D is the right answer.

questions.question: In the below question, sentence/passage is split into four parts and named P, Q, R & S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the sentence/passage and find out which of the four combinations is correct. P. Number 5 Britt Lane took some finding. Q. In this huddle of old houses all kinds of adaptation and conditioning had taken place. R. Stables had become cottages, kitchen wings had become houses, odd storeys had become maisonettes. S. Number 5 Britt Lane seemed to be just a number on a gate.
questions.explanation: Sentences Q and R are a pair: Q describes the cluttered appearance of the streets, while R builds up on it, and tells us how different habitations had come to acquire purposes other than ones they had been originally built for. Options A and D are eliminated. It is clear that the pair QR should follow sentence P, since P tells us about a certain house that was hard to find, and QR follow by telling us the reason for it. Option B is eliminated. Option C is the right answer.

questions.question: In the below question, sentence/passage is split into four parts and named P, Q, R & S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the sentence/passage and find out which of the four combinations is correct. P. Her eyes, a darkish blue, were set wide apart in a face of the type popularly referred to as heart-shaped. Q. She was not very tall, and certainly not pretty. R. The girl was wearing her school coat, and childish low-heeled clumpish black school shoes; and consequently looked younger than Blair had anticipated. S. But she had - what was the word? - appeal.
questions.explanation: Sentences Q and S are a pair: Q describes the plain appearance of the girl, while S builds up on it, and tells us that despite being plain in looks, the girl had a certain appeal. Options A and B are eliminated. It is clear that sentence P should follow the pair QS, since it further builds up on the appearance of the girl. Option C is eliminated. Option D is the right answer.

questions.question: A part of the sentence is bracketed. Below are given alternatives to the bracketed part which may improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative. In case no improvement is required, choose "No Improvement" option. (If we had bought) this book earlier, we would have passed the exam.
questions.explanation: The sentence is grammatically and contextually correct and needs no improvement.

questions.question: A sentence, a part of the sentence is bracketed. Below are given alternatives to the bracketed part which may improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative. In case no improvement is needed choose "No Improvement". Your company has a (believe) that that no one should be allowed to turn up for work late.
questions.explanation: 'Belief' is a noun and 'Believe' is a verb. Also, we cannot use plural noun because of the presence of 'a'.

questions.question: It can be inferred from the passage that the problem of inflation started to haunt India since
options: The increase in the index number of wholesale prices
The outbreak of second world war
The shortfall in the production of food grains.
questions.explanation: From the sentence "The spectre of inflation has been haunting India since the outbreak of the Second World War" in passage we can infer that answer is b.

questions.question: What is the cause of inflation in India?
options: The shortfall in the production of food grains.
questions.explanation: From the last two sentences of the passage we can infer that all these are various causes of inflation.

questions.question: The primary purpose of the passage is to
options: Present how bad the effects of inflation are in Indian economy.
Give awareness about the various causes of inflation.
Study about the various causes of inflation to arrive at the anti-inflationary measures.
questions.explanation: From the sentence "The anti-inflationary methods obviously require the study of the causes of inflation", and the sentences following that which discusses about the various causes of inflation, we can conclude that the answer is option c.

questions.question: A suitable title for the passage is
options: How bad is the inflation in India?
How to reduce inflation in India?
Inflation-Its effects and causes in India Economy
questions.explanation: The passage starts with a definition for inflation, then its effects on Indian economy and ends by discussing about the causes of inflation in Indian economy. Option (a) is too broad and general. The passage does not only focus on how grave the inflation was or how to reduce it. This eliminates options (b) and (c). So, the most suitable title is (d).

questions.question: What does the author mean by the idiom "feel the pinch" used in the sentence "But never has the pinch been felt so severely or so pervasively as during the last five years"?
options: to suffer the adverse effects of something
to experience the effect of having less money
questions.explanation: "To feel the pinch" means to experience difficulties because you are earning less than before. Author uses this idiom to convey the effect high inflation in the last five years.

questions.question: out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentences. One who performs scientific analysis of elections
questions.explanation: Returning officer: The official in each electorate who conducts elections and announces results. Arbitrator: An independent person or body appointed to settle a dispute. Ombudsman: An official appointed to investigate individuals' complaints against a company or organization, especially a public authority Psephologist: One who studies or analyze elections, pattern of voting etc.

questions.question: Out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentences. A collection of information about a particular subject
questions.explanation: Anthology: A collection of literary works like poems, short stories, etc. Bibliophile: A person who loves and collects books. Compendium: A collection of information about a particular subject. Encyclopedia: A book giving information on many subjects.

questions.question: In the following question, a word in capital is followed by four options. From the options, find the appropriate word that reflects the closest or similar meaning (synonym) to the given word. FRACTIOUS
questions.explanation: Fractious means irritable and quarrelsome. Hence, the word closest in meaning is "Grumpy"

questions.question: Four alternatives are given for the idiom/phrase underlined. Choose the correct alternative that best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. Go out on a limb
questions.explanation: 'go out on a limb' means, get into a tough or disadvantaged position in order to support someone. Example: He went out on a limb and expressed an opinion which was opposite to that of his entire class.

questions.question: Choose the word similar in meaning to the given word. Prologue
questions.explanation: Prologue is a separate introductory section of a literary, dramatic, or musical work. Hence, 'Introduction' is similar in meaning.

questions.question: Choose the word opposite in meaning to the given word. Indolent
questions.explanation: Indolent means lazy. So the word opposite in meaning is "Active"

questions.question: Four alternatives are given for the idiom/phrase. Choose the correct alternative that best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. Hole and corner activities
questions.explanation: Hole and corner activities refer to those which are held secret because they are dishonest.

questions.question: Choose the word opposite in meaning to the given word. Dubious
questions.explanation: Dubious means doubtful or uncertain. Thus 'Certain' is opposite in meaning

questions.question: Sentences given with blanks to be filled in with an appropriate word(s). Four alternatives are suggested for each question. Choose the correct alternative out of the four. No sooner did she read the message _________ she fainted.
questions.explanation: The correct grammatical structure is 'No sooner + Auxiliary verb........than..'. Hence, the correct choice is 'than'.

questions.question: Sentences given with blanks to be filled in with an appropriate word(s). Four alternatives are suggested for each question. Choose the correct alternative out of the four. It is welcome that the Centre has announced a package of measures to _________ investment, employment and exports in this sector.
questions.explanation: The word 'boast' means to speak excessively for. 'Boasted' is the past form of this verb. Hence, both options are incorrect. 'Burst' means to break open or break apart suddenly and violently. 'Boost' that means give impetus to grow, fits in the sentence and hence, is the correct choice.

questions.question: Four words are given in each question, out of which only word is correctly spelt. Find the correctly spelt word and mark your answer in the answer sheet
questions.explanation: Solution: The correctly spelt word is: Referred. The correct spelling of other words is as follows- Accommodate, Cemetery, Rhythm.
